summary.summary: Adds a settings-search scroll-and-highlight feature: a new view modifier SettingsSearchHighlight (exposed as .settingsHighlightScroll(…)) that pairs a settings row with a search target, scrolls to the matched control when navigating into a settings sub-screen, and animates a brief transient highlight (pr:gordolio/Trio#1165, pr:nightscout/Trio#1165, diff:ac5cc3a5e5bf). The modifier was applied across the searchable settings surfaces and SettingItem gained scrollTargetLabels mapping entries so matches that are not themselves visible rows can route to a visible parent row (examples in the PR description) (pr:gordolio/Trio#1165, file:bd6abb72a508, diff:ac5cc3a5e5bf). Unit tests were added to cover mapping resolution (SettingsSearchTests.swift) (pr:gordolio/Trio#1165). The change is delivered as part of a merge commit that also includes multiple project, build, and resource updates (including additions to the Xcode project and Config.xcconfig entries) (commit:588483b8a5736d7d7e4367dcb81d6faeed1db88e, commit:ca73ca60493accd2dc0975af3b569ed8430ef4ac, file:108405f67777, file:f074d545f111, diff:ac5cc3a5e5bf). The same merge also adds telemetry-related source files and privacy/telemetry documentation text (e.g., TelemetryClient additions and expanded PRIVACY_POLICY.md text describing anonymous usage telemetry) (file:ac4d3c53e0ac, diff:ac5cc3a5e5bf).
summary.userFacingImpact: Search results within Settings will now navigate directly to the matched control and briefly highlight it, reducing the need for manual scanning after tapping a search result (pr:gordolio/Trio#1165, diff:ac5cc3a5e5bf). The merge also introduces telemetry-related UI/code and privacy-policy text describing an "Anonymous Usage Telemetry" opt-in flow (file:ac4d3c53e0ac, diff:ac5cc3a5e5bf); because telemetry and privacy changes can have broader user-impact and deployment implications, a human review is recommended to confirm defaults, opt-in behavior, and any backend dependencies before release (diff:ac5cc3a5e5bf).
